source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Sit correctly at a table, holding a pencil comfortably and correctly; begin to form lower-case letters in the correct direction, starting and finishing in the right place.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack treats handwriting as an optional, supportive tracing interaction with motor-access alternatives.

objective.child_goal: I can form lowercase letters with the right start and direction.
objective.statement: Form selected lowercase letters using correct start point, direction and finish point, with audio and visual support.
misconceptions: Starts letters from the bottom when they should start at the top.
Forms letters in the wrong direction.
Focuses only on the final shape and ignores movement path.

vocabulary.audio_script: The start point tells your pencil where to begin.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A glowing dot at the start of a letter.

vocabulary.audio_script: Direction is the path your pencil follows.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A trail arrow around a letter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Follow the glowing path with your finger.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Follow the path slowly. Start at the glowing dot.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Large letter path with animated start dot.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The letter trail shows where to start and which way to go.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce handwriting as movement, not only shape.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Letters have a start, a path and a finish.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A trail marker moves around a lowercase letter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: For c, start near the top and curve around.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a simple curved letter path.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Start near the top. Curve around like a small moon.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Letter c drawn with a soft trail.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Trace the letter path, then try again with less help.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Stay close to the trail. It is okay to try again.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Trace canvas with wide tolerance and replay button.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap where this letter should start.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check start point without requiring motor tracing.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Find the start point before you trace.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three possible start dots around a letter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why the start point matters.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I started at the bottom and got muddled. Can you show me the start?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares two letter paths.

manipulatives.purpose: Support letter formation with visible start points, direction arrows and motor-access alternatives.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners tap start points, trace paths, replay audio and use non-tracing choices where fine-motor support is needed.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Wide tolerance, reduced-motion static arrows, keyboard/tap alternatives and no punitive scoring for motor control.

manipulatives.purpose: Teach where a lowercase letter begins and which way it travels without requiring a tracing gesture.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose the correct start-point firefly, then select direction arrows in order; spoken letter and direction prompts use ElevenLabs assets only after a human listener has approved them.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets support keyboard focus, single-switch scanning and tap; static mode shows numbered arrows, while reduced-motion mode uses colour-independent focus rings instead of flying fireflies.

practice.interleaving_notes: Group letters by movement family before mixed retrieval.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify start points for lowercase movement families.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: letter + three start dots + audio prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include motor-access alternative for all trace tasks.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Trace lowercase letters with fading visual support.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: letter path + start dot + tolerance band + replay
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Needs device testing on touchscreens and trackpads.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose correct movement direction without requiring writing.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two trace animations/static arrows + choose correct path

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain start and direction knowledge through spaced review.
Connect letter formation to simple CVC word writing where appropriate.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: target letter in word + trace path + sound prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not require word writing for children needing fine-motor alternatives.

body.prompt: Tap where lowercase c should start.

hints: Look for the glowing dot.
The c starts near the top.
question_variants.explanation: Lowercase c starts near the top and curves around.

body.prompt: Which path forms c correctly?
choices: start at bottom and go up

hints: Listen to the path.
The c curves around.
question_variants.explanation: The correct c path starts near the top and curves around.

body.prompt: Trace lowercase c along the trail.

hints: Start at the dot.
Move slowly around the curve.
question_variants.explanation: A good c starts near the top and follows the curved path.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Starts letters from the bottom when they should start at the top.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Trace path begins from the wrong start zone or start-point choice is incorrect.
repair_sequence: Ask learner to tap start before tracing.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses the correct start point for a new letter.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Focuses only on the final shape and ignores movement path.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Final trace resembles letter but uses inefficient or reversed movement.
repair_sequence: Compare smooth path with muddled path.
Practise start and direction without scoring shape.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner follows correct path direction on a new attempt.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use soft trails and no flashing correction marks.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static arrows and step markers.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use letters, sounds and icons instead of written instructions.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Practise one letter movement at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited replay and no timer.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Separate motor effort from learning judgement.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach start, finish, curve, line, round and trace with gestures.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires correct start and direction for target letters; secure requires retained start/direction knowledge with motor-access alternatives respected.
evidence.parent_summary: Can start and form selected lowercase letters in the right direction with support.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Identifies start points, follows letter paths and uses correct formation language.
next_actions: Connect letter formation to phonics only when motor load is appropriate.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 1 lowercase-letter-formation pack with a deterministic 180-item pilot bank. Three curated variants are preserved alongside candidates covering recognition, start points, movement paths, orientation, formation families, b/d/p/q and other discrimination support, and transfer to letters within familiar words. Generated tasks separate formation knowledge from fine-motor performance through visual, verbal, tactile, switch, eye-gaze and path-choice alternatives. Referenced narration follows the ElevenLabs policy: browser TTS is prohibited and every asset remains unavailable pending human listening review. Joyful quest progress rewards noticing and choosing strategies, never speed, precision-only scoring or streaks. Handwriting-style, phonics, motor-access, teacher, renderer and human-listening review remains required before promotion.
